qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Crafted PSD layer extents bypass decoder bounds checks and crash Pillow
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a FITS GZIP decompression-bomb bug in src/PIL/FitsImagePlugin.py caused by unbounded decompression/read in FitsGzipDecoder.decode, while this finding is a PSD layer-bounds integer-overflow/out-of-bounds-write crash in src/PIL/PsdImagePlugin.py and src/decode.c.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's FITS GZIP decompression path or its unbounded-read root cause. The only real qualifying finding in this session is a separate PSD decoder memory-safety crash.
evidence: The advisory summary and patch describe a denial-of-service issue in FITS GZIP decoding where Pillow failed to limit decompressed data reads in FitsGzipDecoder.decode within src/PIL/FitsImagePlugin.py.

The only final qualifying finding in .judge/input_manifest.json is psd_tile_oob_write, and its report ties the bug to PSD layer bounding boxes flowing through src/PIL/PsdImagePlugin.py, ImageFile.setimage, and src/decode.c.

The psd_tile_oob_write PoC reproduces a segmentation fault while loading a crafted PSD layer, which demonstrates a different parser, file format, sink, and root cause than the advisory-described FITS decompression bomb.
